title: Professional Certifications Guide for College Students
source: Compiled from CFA Institute, AICPA, PMI, CompTIA, NACE, and others

certifications.name: CFA (Chartered Financial Analyst)
certifications.levels: Three exam levels (Level I, II, III)
certifications.requirements: Bachelor's degree (or final year student can register for Level I), 4,000 hours of relevant work experience for charter
certifications.salaryImpact: CFA charterholders earn median $180,000+ (varies significantly by role and location)
certifications.studentTip: Start studying in junior/senior year. Pass Level I before or just after graduation to stand out in investment banking, asset management, and equity research roles.

certifications.name: CPA (Certified Public Accountant)
certifications.levels: Four exam sections (AUD, BEC, FAR, REG)
certifications.requirements: 150 credit hours of education (most states), bachelor's degree, work experience varies by state
certifications.salaryImpact: CPAs earn 10-15% more than non-CPA accountants. Starting salary: $55,000-$70,000 at Big 4.
certifications.studentTip: The 150 credit hour requirement often means a 5th year or master's. Plan this early. Big 4 firms often prefer candidates who are "CPA eligible" at graduation.

certifications.name: CompTIA A+
certifications.requirements: No formal prerequisites (recommended: 9-12 months IT experience)
certifications.salaryImpact: Entry IT roles: $40,000-$55,000. The cert helps get your foot in the door.
certifications.studentTip: Best entry-level IT cert. Earn it during college to qualify for help desk and IT support roles. Pairs well with any major.

certifications.name: CompTIA Security+
certifications.requirements: CompTIA A+ or Network+ recommended (not required)
certifications.salaryImpact: Cybersecurity analyst starting salary: $65,000-$85,000. Required for many DoD positions.
certifications.studentTip: Cybersecurity has a massive talent shortage. This cert plus a bachelor's degree makes you very competitive. Required for government/defense cybersecurity work.

certifications.name: AWS Cloud Practitioner
certifications.salaryImpact: AWS-certified professionals earn 25-30% more on average in tech roles
certifications.studentTip: Cloud skills are in massive demand. Start with Cloud Practitioner, then pursue Solutions Architect Associate. AWS offers free training and student credits.

certifications.name: Google Data Analytics Certificate
certifications.salaryImpact: Data analysts earn median $82,000 (BLS). The cert helps career changers and supplements any major.
certifications.studentTip: This is one of the best bang-for-buck credentials. Teaches SQL, R, Tableau, and data visualization. Pairs excellently with business, social science, or health majors.

certifications.name: CAPM (Certified Associate in Project Management)
certifications.requirements: 23 hours of project management education (free options available)
certifications.salaryImpact: Project coordinator/analyst starting: $50,000-$65,000. PMP holders earn median $116,000.
certifications.studentTip: Great addition to ANY major. Project management skills are needed everywhere. Earn CAPM in college, then get PMP after 3 years of work experience.

certifications.name: SHRM-CP
certifications.requirements: HR-related bachelor's degree or 1 year HR experience
certifications.salaryImpact: HR specialists with SHRM-CP earn $60,000-$75,000 starting
certifications.studentTip: If you're interested in people-focused business roles, HR is growing and well-compensated. SHRM offers student membership and resources.

naceSalaryData.source: NACE (National Association of Colleges and Employers)
naceSalaryData.description: NACE tracks starting salary offers for new college graduates by major.
